data.title: Pacifica Silver launches $10M brokered private placement
data.description:
• Pacifica Silver (PAGFF) has entered into a brokered private placement agreement led by Raymond James for up to 6.9M common shares priced at $1.45/share.
  • The offering is expected to generate gross proceeds of up to ~$10M.
  • Agents have been granted a 15% option to sell up to an additional 1.04M shares at the same price.
  • Proceeds will be used to advance exploration and drilling at the Claudia Project in Durango, Mexico.
  • Funds will also support working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Bentley Systems repays $678 million convertible notes at maturity
data.description:
• Bentley Systems (Nasdaq: BSY) on Thursday said that it has repaid $678 million in principal and accrued interest on its 0.125% convertible senior notes due 2026.
  • The repayment was funded with cash on hand and $610 million drawn from the company’s revolving credit facilities.
  • Bentley said $575 million of 0.375% convertible senior notes due in mid-2027 remain outstanding.
  • The company said retiring the notes reduces its fully diluted share count by more than 10 million shares, or about 3%.
  • BSY +0.02% after hours to $39.06.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Silver North plans C$5 mln brokered private placement
data.description:
• Silver North Resources (SNAG:CA) said on Thursday it has agreed with Red Cloud Securities to conduct a brokered “best efforts” private placement for gross proceeds of up to C$5.0 million.
  • The offering will consist of up to 5.0 million units priced at C$0.40 each and up to 5.36 million charitable flow-through units priced at C$0.56 each. Each unit will include one common share and one-half of a share purchase warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable at C$0.56 for up to 36 months after closing.
  • Red Cloud has been granted an option to sell up to an additional C$1.0 million of units or flow-through units.
  • Proceeds will be used for exploration and related programs at the company’s Haldane and Veronica properties in Yukon, as well as for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The company expects the offering to close on or about February 5, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals, including acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • TARSF closed -1.62% at $0.3386.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sea extends losses for the seventh straight session
data.description:
Sea (SE) extended its decline to a seventh consecutive session, as the company’s shares closed 1.07% lower at $123.91 on Thursday.

Over the prior six sessions, the stock fell more than 12.35%, underperforming the S&P 500, which declined about 0.26%. On a year-to-date basis, the shares are down over 4.2%, compared with a roughly 1.19% gain in the broader S&P 500 Index in 2025.

According to Seeking Alpha Analyst Narek Hovhannisyan, SE Q3 results showed strong operating momentum, with record e-commerce GMV and order volumes, 38% revenue growth, and profitability across all business segments. Net profit, however, fell short of market expectations due to higher tax expenses. Despite recent weakness in the share price, the stock is now trading below its historical valuation levels, supported by a strong balance sheet with net cash, negative net debt, and a recently announced $1 billion share buyback program.

Further, he added key risks include intense e-commerce competition, emerging market macro volatility, and regulatory/tax headwinds, including intense competition in the e-commerce space, macroeconomic volatility across key emerging markets, and potential regulatory or tax headwinds.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating,  SE has a Hold rating, with a score of 2.77 out of 5. The stock carries a D+ rating for valuation,  A+ for growth, and A for profitability, while momentum is rated D.

However, Wall Street analysts  remain largely bullish, with 31 rating the stock Buy or higher, two assigning a Hold, and none recommending a Sell.

Similarly, Seeking Alpha’s analysts also support a Buy view on the stock, assigning it a rating of 3.75 out of 5.

Another Seeking Alpha analyst, Fundamental Options, has given a Buy rating on Sea, citing about 39% upside to a fair value of $169.5, supported by a GARP-based approach and continued strength in e-commerce growth.

While Shopee remains the market leader in Southeast Asia with a 52% market share, the analyst noted that the company's overall competitive moat is relatively narrow and profitability remains modest amid intense competition.

data.title: Wall Street ended higher as chip stocks climbed and Trump paused critical mineral tariffs
data.description:
Wall Street ended higher on Thursday as investors reacted positively to President Donald Trump’s decision to delay tariffs on critical minerals, alongside his assurance that he does not intend to remove Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed on top by 0.2%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added 0.2% on the day, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished higher by 0.6%. 

From a sector vantage point, seven of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors ended in positive trading territory, with utilities leading the charge. Going in the other direction, energy suffered the most as oil prices fell 4.9%.

"Because of TSMC’s good earnings and tech’s recent underperformance (the Russell 2000 had the best win streak compared to the S&P 500 since 1990), some called it bargain hunting for tech today. However, the equal-weight index still managed to beat the S&P 500, with support from higher-yielding dividend stocks and general cyclical value. It seems that the market has woken up to favorable valuation differences, which I expect to last, especially if we get a broader-based economic recovery," Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen noted.

Treasury yields edged higher across the curve as investors sold bonds. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 5 basis points to 3.56%, while the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) ticked up 3 basis points to 4.16%. 

On the economic calendar front, initial jobless claims dipped by 9K to 198K for the week ended Jan. 10, which was lower than the 215K forecasted level. Moreover, the Empire State Manufacturing Index came in at 7.7 versus the 1.0 consensus level, according to data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) ended higher by 4.5%, and Boston Scientific (BSX) fell 4% on the session.

data.title: Vaalco Energy surges after touting two positive wells offshore Gabon
data.description:
Vaalco Energy (EGY) +5.3% in Thursday's trading after saying it drilled two positive wells at its Etame complex offshore Gabon.

Vaalco (EGY) said the initial well in its Phase 3 multi-well development drilling program in its Etame license hit high-quality reservoir sands in the Gamba formation after being drilled to a total depth of 2,397 meters in the western Etame-1V fault block.

The company said pressure data confirmed "strong communication" with nearby producing wells, supporting the presence of a connected and productive reservoir system with initial estimates of 2.4M-3.2M barrels of oil in place.

Vaalco (EGY) also said a second pilot well was drilled to a depth of 2,175 meters on the western side of the main Etame fault block, also targeting the Gamba, intercepting "multiple high-quality sand intervals."

While Vaalco (EGY) conducts further analysis of the findings to determine future commercial viability of the resource. it said it is drilling a horizontal production sidetrack off the first well, which is due to start producing in the current quarter.

data.title: Goldman considers entry into prediction markets, David Solomon says
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) is exploring prediction markets and how it can benefit from this fast-growing but lightly regulated market, the firm's chief executive said on Thursday.

“We have a team of people here that are spending time with them and are looking at it,” CEO David Solomon said during the investment bank's Q4 earnings call.

In the last two weeks, Solomon has personally met with the two big prediction firms (POLYMARKET) (KALSHI) and their leadership "and spent a couple of hours with each to learn more about" these markets.

Goldman's (GS) potential entrance into prediction markets would bring the company deeper into competition with the likes of retail trading app Robinhood Markets (HOOD), which saw its stock retreat 7.6% in Thursday midafternoon trading. If Goldman -- one of the largest Wall Street banks -- moves in, how would Robinhood compete and sustain momentum in that space?

Solomon said that he sees potential where prediction market contracts regulated by the Commodity Futures trading Commission "cross into our business,” while warning that adoption is likely to be slower than some expect.

GS shares climbed 4.3% after its Q4 earnings beat the consensus estimate, although its revenue missed Wall Street expectations due to markdowns related to its pending sale of the Apple credit card portfolio to JPMorgan Chase.

data.title: Mega-to-large cap information technology stocks with the weakest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off, a review of mega to large cap information technology stocks reveals several companies currently receiving the weakest grades for earnings momentum.

This analysis examines stocks arranged according to their EPS revision grades, highlighting those at the bottom of the scale.

The focus centers on companies that have earned an ‘F’ grade for EPS revisions, indicating particularly weak earnings momentum. This grade reflects significant downward pressure on analyst expectations for future earnings.

Here are the info tech stocks with the lowest EPS revision grades:

  1. Hon Hai Precision Industry (HNHPF) — EPS Revision Grade: F
  2. Infineon Technologies AG (IFNNY) — EPS Revision Grade: F
  3. Kyocera (KYOCY) — EPS Revision Grade: F 
  4. Lenovo Group (LNVGY) — EPS Revision Grade: F 
  5. F5 (FFIV) — EPS Revision Grade: D-
  6. FUJIFILM Holdings (FUJIY) — EPS Revision Grade: D- 
  7. Hexagon AB (HXGBY) — EPS Revision Grade: D-
  8. Nutanix (NTNX) — EPS Revision Grade: D-
  9. Roper Technologies (ROP) — EPS Revision Grade: D-
  10. Super Micro Computer (SMCI) — EPS Revision Grade: D-

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.description:

data.title: UBS sees containerboard rebound in 2026, favors International Paper, Smurfit WestRock
data.description:
UBS analysts are growing more constructive on the North American containerboard sector heading into 2026, citing tightening supply, improving demand fundamentals and the potential for a successful price increase after a weak 2025.

In a Jan. 14 outlook report, UBS said unprecedented capacity reductions across the industry -- roughly 4 million tons, or about 12% of 2024 demand -- should help support at least one containerboard price hike in 2026. The firm forecasts a $40-per-ton increase in the second quarter, arguing that pricing skepticism tied to soft box shipments and weak recycled fiber prices may be overstated.

UBS expects volumes to stabilize and gradually recover next year as lower interest rates and business- and consumer-friendly tax policies lift manufacturing activity and spending. The firm’s proprietary “UBS Corrugator” model points to roughly 70 basis points of year-over-year box shipment growth in 2026, with industrial production and consumer demand acting as the primary drivers.

Stock picks

Against that backdrop, UBS reiterated Buy ratings on International Paper (IP) and Smurfit WestRock (SW) (SMFTF) (SMFKY), calling both stocks its preferred ways to play a containerboard recovery.

For International Paper (IP), UBS highlighted self-help initiatives and cost savings following recent mill closures, as well as the company’s long-term target of $5 billion in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization by 2027. The bank set a $51 price target on the stock, implying roughly 20% upside from current levels even under conservative margin assumptions.

Smurfit WestRock, meanwhile, is seen benefiting from $400 million in announced synergies, with potential for another $400 million over time. UBS noted that upcoming strategy updates could provide greater clarity on execution and incremental savings.

By contrast, UBS struck a more cautious tone on consumer boxboard, maintaining Neutral ratings on Graphic Packaging Holding (GPK) and Sonoco Products (SON).

The firm warned that boxboard markets remain oversupplied, with additional pressure coming from the expansion of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs, particularly in pill form, which UBS believes could weigh on packaged food demand. For Graphic Packaging (GPK), UBS flagged pricing risk tied to historically low spreads between coated recycled board and substitute grades, alongside volume headwinds as the company ramps a new mill in Waco, Texas.

Sonoco (SON) is expected to benefit from prior footprint rationalization and price increases in industrial boxboard, but UBS noted that divestitures and portfolio shifts could offset those gains in 2026, resulting in modest earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization pressure year over year.

Overall, UBS said the balance of risks in 2026 favors containerboard producers over boxboard names, with supply discipline and macro tailwinds creating a more supportive environment for pricing and earnings recovery than investors currently expect.
